title: Classical Composers & Works
description: Famous classical music compositions and their composers

cards.front: Messiah (oratorio featuring the 'Hallelujah Chorus')
Handel's most famous oratorio featuring the 'Hallelujah Chorus'
The Four Seasons (set of violin concertos)
Vivaldi's most famous set of four violin concertos representing spring, summer, autumn, and winter
The Nutcracker (ballet)
Tchaikovsky's Christmas ballet featuring the Sugar Plum Fairy and the Dance of the Snowflakes
Swan Lake (ballet)
Tchaikovsky's ballet about a princess turned into a swan by an evil sorcerer
The Magic Flute (opera)
Mozart's fairy-tale opera featuring the Queen of the Night aria
The Marriage of Figaro (opera)
Mozart's comic opera based on a play by Beaumarchais about a servant outwitting his master
1812 Overture
Tchaikovsky's overture commemorating Russia's defense against Napoleon, famous for its use of real cannons
Moonlight Sonata (Piano Sonata No. 14)
Beethoven's piano sonata nicknamed for its dreamy, moonlit first movement
Für Elise
Beethoven's short piano piece, one of the most recognized classical melodies in the world
Symphony No. 9 (featuring 'Ode to Joy')
Beethoven's final symphony, the first major symphony to include a choral finale ('Ode to Joy')
The Rite of Spring (ballet)
Stravinsky's revolutionary ballet that caused a riot at its 1913 Paris premiere
Carmen (opera)
Bizet's opera set in Seville about a fiery gypsy woman and a soldier
Rhapsody in Blue
Gershwin's jazz-influenced orchestral work that opens with a famous clarinet glissando
Boléro
Ravel's orchestral piece built on a single repeating melody that grows gradually louder
The Planets (orchestral suite)
Holst's seven-movement orchestral suite with movements like 'Mars, the Bringer of War' and 'Jupiter, the Bringer of Jollity'
William Tell Overture
Rossini's overture famous for its finale used as the theme of The Lone Ranger
Ride of the Valkyries
Wagner's dramatic orchestral passage from the opera Die Walküre, often associated with helicopters in film
Canon in D
Pachelbel's most famous composition, commonly played at weddings
Water Music (orchestral suites)
Handel's orchestral suites composed for King George I's barge party on the River Thames
The Well-Tempered Clavier
Bach's collection of preludes and fugues in all 24 major and minor keys
Symphony No. 5 (famous four-note opening: da-da-da-DUM)
Beethoven's symphony famous for its four-note 'fate knocking at the door' opening motif
New World Symphony (Symphony No. 9)
Dvořák's symphony inspired by his time in America, incorporating Native American and African American musical themes
Clair de Lune
Debussy's dreamy piano piece whose title means 'moonlight' in French
Eine kleine Nachtmusik (A Little Night Music)
Mozart's popular serenade whose German title translates to 'A Little Night Music'
The Barber of Seville (opera)
Rossini's comic opera about the clever barber Figaro helping Count Almaviva win Rosina's love
Aida (opera)
Verdi's grand opera set in ancient Egypt, premiered in Cairo in 1871
La traviata (opera)
Verdi's opera about a Parisian courtesan who sacrifices her love; its title means 'The Fallen Woman'
Toccata and Fugue in D Minor (organ)
Bach's dramatic organ work often associated with haunted houses and horror films
cards.back: Toccata and Fugue in D Minor

cards.front: Hungarian Rhapsody No. 2
Liszt's virtuosic piano showpiece inspired by Hungarian folk music, famously used in Tom and Jerry cartoons
Symphony No. 94 ('Surprise Symphony')
Haydn's symphony nicknamed 'Surprise' because of a sudden loud chord meant to wake up dozing audience members
Ave Maria (adapted from Bach's Prelude in C)
Gounod's vocal setting of a prayer layered over a Bach prelude, often performed at weddings
The Firebird (ballet)
Stravinsky's first major ballet, based on a Russian fairy tale about a magical glowing bird
Sleeping Beauty (ballet)
Tchaikovsky's ballet based on the Charles Perrault fairy tale about a princess cursed to sleep for 100 years
Peer Gynt Suite (incidental music)
Grieg's orchestral suites featuring 'Morning Mood' and 'In the Hall of the Mountain King'
Nocturnes (set of lyrical piano pieces)
Chopin's genre of lyrical, dreamy piano pieces meant to evoke the night
Minute Waltz (Waltz in D-flat Major, Op. 64 No. 1)
Chopin's famous short waltz nicknamed for being playable in about one minute
Lullaby (Wiegenlied, Op. 49 No. 4)
Brahms's gentle song that became one of the world's most famous lullabies
Symphony No. 40 in G Minor
Mozart's dramatic symphony in G minor, one of only two he wrote in a minor key
Trout Quintet (Piano Quintet in A Major)
Schubert's chamber work nicknamed for its fourth movement based on his song 'The Trout'
Ave Maria (D. 839)
Schubert's setting of a prayer from Walter Scott's 'The Lady of the Lake,' one of the most beloved sacred songs
Requiem in D Minor (K. 626)
Mozart's final composition, left unfinished at his death in 1791
